title: Boxy Configuration
description: Configuration file for Boxy sandboxing orchestration tool.

pools.description: Resource pool definitions. Each pool maintains a warm set of pre-provisioned resources.

agents.description: Remote agent configurations for distributed resource management.

backend.description: Provider backend to use for provisioning resources.

image.description: Image or template to use when provisioning resources. Required for container and vm types.

min_ready.description: Minimum number of resources to keep in the ready state (warm pool size).

max_total.description: Maximum total resources allowed (ready + allocated + provisioning).

cpus.description: Number of CPUs to allocate per resource.

memory_mb.description: Memory allocation per resource in megabytes.

disk_gb.description: Disk allocation per resource in gigabytes (primarily for VMs).

labels.description: Key-value labels to apply to resources in this pool.

environment.description: Environment variables to set on resources in this pool.

health_check_interval.description: Interval between health checks. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '30s', '5m').

AgentConfig.description: Configuration for a remote Boxy agent.

address.description: Network address of the agent in host:port format.

providers.description: List of provider names available on this agent.

tls_cert_path.description: Path to the client TLS certificate file.

tls_key_path.description: Path to the client TLS key file.

tls_ca_path.description: Path to the CA certificate file for verifying the agent.

use_tls.description: Enable TLS for communication with this agent.

StorageConfig.description: Storage backend configuration for persisting state.

path.description: File path for the SQLite database.

dsn.description: Data source name for PostgreSQL connections.

listen.description: Address and port to listen on (e.g. ':8080').

read_timeout_secs.description: HTTP server read timeout in seconds.

write_timeout_secs.description: HTTP server write timeout in seconds.

idle_timeout_secs.description: HTTP keep-alive idle timeout in seconds.

HookConfig.description: Lifecycle hook configuration for a pool.

on_provision.description: Hooks that run after a resource is provisioned (background finalization).

on_allocate.description: Hooks that run before a resource is allocated to a user (fast personalization).

Hook.description: A lifecycle hook that runs a script at a specific point in the resource lifecycle.

shell.description: Shell interpreter to use for running the script.

inline.description: Inline script content to execute. Mutually exclusive with 'path'.

path.description: Path to a script file to execute. Mutually exclusive with 'inline'.

timeout.description: Maximum execution time for this hook. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '30s', '5m').

retry.description: Number of retry attempts if the hook fails.

continue_on_failure.description: If true, hook failure does not prevent the lifecycle operation from completing.

TimeoutConfig.description: Phase-level timeout configuration for pool lifecycle operations.

provision.description: Maximum time for the provider to provision a resource. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '5m').

finalization.description: Maximum combined time for all on_provision hooks. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '10m').

personalization.description: Maximum combined time for all on_allocate hooks. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '30s').

destroy.description: Maximum time for the provider to destroy a resource. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '1m').
